definition: The $900B-$1.1T in bonds and loans issued at 3-5% (HY) and 2-3% (IG) that must refinance at 7-9% and 5-6% respectively during 2025-2027, creating an average 400bp coupon step-up yielding ~$36B incremental annual interest expense — a 14% increase for the HY universe and 3-5pp FCF margin erosion.
evidence: Average 400bp coupon step-up on $900B yields ~$36B incremental annual interest expense
14% increase for HY universe, 3-5pp FCF margin erosion
Borrowers at true 7.5-8.5x leverage have debt service consuming 70-80% of actual cash EBITDA at 500bp+ OAS
Forces binary outcome: refinance at higher costs (equity repricing) or default (credit repricing)
Survived all four debates without challenge
open_questions: What will be the rate/spread environment at refinancing time?
How many simultaneous defaults can the Kalecki fiscal floor absorb during the maturity wall window?

definition: Credit markets generally lead equity markets by 2-6 months in fundamental deteriorations, but the direction and magnitude of the lead-lag is regime-dependent: credit leads in fundamental deteriorations (2007-08 canonical case, 6-8 month lead), while equities lead or move simultaneously in sentiment/exogenous shocks (2020, 2018 Q4). The direction of lead-lag is diagnostic of the shock type.
evidence: 5 of 6 major episodes since 1990 show credit leading or moving simultaneously with equity
2007-08 canonical case shows 6-8 month credit lead
Challenger_01 concedes 2007 case and shows simultaneous movement in 4 of 7 episodes — still supports credit does not lag claim
Direction of lead-lag is diagnostic of shock type (credit_analyst_01 formulation)
open_questions: Are passive/ETF flows compressing lead time toward zero?
Has algorithmic trading fundamentally altered the lead-lag structure post-2015?
Does high-frequency lead-lag analysis across pre-2015 vs post-2015 show compression to less than 1 month?

definition: EBITDA addbacks of 15-40% and covenant-lite structures (>90% of leveraged loans) create hidden leverage where true leverage is 7.5-8.5x vs reported ~6x. Covenant-lite delays default recognition by 12-24 months but increases loss-given-default, producing a 'fewer but larger' default pattern with estimated recoveries of 40-50 cents vs historical 60-70 cents.
evidence: ECB and Fed regulatory analyses document the 15-40% addback range
>90% of leveraged loans lack maintenance covenants
True leverage of 7.5-8.5x vs reported ~6x
Default models trained on pre-2010 covenanted data are miscalibrated for current universe
Covenant-lite delays default 12-24 months but increases loss-given-default
Merton distance-to-default is significantly narrower than reported financials indicate
Survived all debates
open_questions: What is the precise Merton distance-to-default adjustment for addback-distorted leverage?
By how much do observable credit metrics understate true cycle positioning?
How should default models be recalibrated for the covenant-lite universe?

definition: Current HY OAS of 350-420bp is unambiguously below historical stress thresholds, sitting at the 40th-55th percentile historically and below the minimum threshold for equity stress in every historical analogue. Any shift toward the 450-600bp zone would be significant.
evidence: Below minimum threshold for equity stress in every historical analogue
40th-55th percentile historically
Consensus across all 8 agents — no agent disputes this
open_questions: When and what will trigger movement toward the 450-600bp zone?
Is current tightness a Minsky speculative-phase signal or rational pricing of the fiscal buffer?

definition: Credit-equity correlation varies dramatically by regime: ~0.3-0.4 in normal periods, ~0.7-0.9 during crises (active only ~7% of calendar time or 30 of 420 months since 1990). Critically, correlation drops to 0.3-0.5 during early stress before snapping to 0.8-0.9 during acute stress, meaning the Merton model is least reliable precisely when needed most.
evidence: Normal-period correlation ~0.3-0.4; crisis correlation ~0.7-0.9
Crisis contagion regime active only ~7% of calendar time (30 of 420 months since 1990)
Positive co-movement regime active ~49% of time
Correlation drops to 0.3-0.5 during early stress before snapping to 0.8-0.9 during acute stress
Risk models using constant correlations systematically misprice tail risk
Portfolios designed for crisis correlation are suboptimal 85% of the time
open_questions: Can the correlation instability at regime transitions be used as a real-time diagnostic tool?
What triggers the snap from early-stress decorrelation to acute-stress high correlation?

definition: The Kalecki-Levy identity mechanically channels fiscal deficits (currently 6-7% of GDP) to corporate profits, simultaneously sustaining interest coverage ratios (supporting credit quality) and the P/E denominator (supporting equity valuations). The system's current resilience is fiscally contingent, not structurally earned. Fiscal consolidation exceeding 2pp GDP would trigger credit cycle downshift within 4-6 quarters.
evidence: Kalecki-Levy identity mechanically channels fiscal deficits to corporate profits
Pair_2 debate identified this as the most important consensus finding
Fiscal consolidation exceeding 2pp GDP would trigger credit cycle downshift within 4-6 quarters per Minsky-Kalecki framework
Artificial decorrelation between credit spreads and equity risk premia is a Minsky speculative phase signal
Kalecki floor is sufficient for IG/upper HY but insufficient for lower HY/leveraged loans where firm-level interest expense overwhelms aggregate demand support
open_questions: Can fiscal deficits indefinitely mask credit deterioration?
What is the interaction between micro credit stress (maturity wall at firm level) and macro profit support (fiscal deficits at aggregate level)?
Is the threshold number of simultaneous defaults required to overwhelm the Kalecki floor reachable during the maturity wall window?

definition: CRE-to-bank-equity contagion operates through a multi-stage regional bank balance sheet mechanism: mark-to-market losses → provisioning → lending pullback. Regional banks hold 28-30% CRE concentration vs 6-8% for largest banks, across ~$2.7T total bank CRE exposure, with office values down 25-40% from peak. However, the Japan model (slow extend-and-pretend) is more probable than sudden cascade given 13-14% CET1 ratios.
evidence: ~$2.7T bank CRE exposure
Regional banks at 28-30% CRE concentration vs 6-8% for largest banks
Office down 25-40% from peak
1990 and 2023 as templates
Median 38% equity drawdown with banking channel active vs 22% without
Duration 18-30 months with banking channel vs 3-6 months without
Joint probability of full contagion cascade is 3-8% per challenger_01
KRE persistently shorted since March 2023, CMBS BBB- at 800-1200bp — may be priced consensus
open_questions: Is CRE-bank contagion now priced consensus rather than genuine tail risk?
Will Japan model slow extend-and-pretend be the resolution?
Is the risk-reward for trading this thesis already unfavorable even if the analytical mechanism is correct?
Will Stage 3 (lending pullback) materialize or be prevented by regulatory forbearance?

definition: The $1.7T private credit market represents an unprecedented opacity risk: mark-to-model practices, quarterly reporting lags, manager-determined NAVs, and exclusion from all public indices and financial conditions measures. PIK toggle usage at 5-8% (up from ~2% in 2022). Three identified spillover channels: semi-liquid vehicle redemption cascades (BDCs/interval funds, ~$300B), insurance company RBC rebalancing (~$700B exposure), and information contagion from first forced mark-to-market. The highest-importance, lowest-precision risk in credit-equity linkage.
evidence: Mark-to-model practices, quarterly reporting lags, manager-determined NAVs
PIK toggle usage at 5-8% up from ~2% in 2022
Outside all public indices, default statistics, and financial conditions measures
No historical analogue at this scale
Base rate framework from 5 analogues (S&Ls, SIVs, junk bonds, Japanese jusen, AT1) shows 60-80% disorderly repricing rate with 4-5 year median lag
Three spillover channels: BDC/interval fund redemptions (~$300B), insurance RBC (~$700B), information contagion
Mark-to-recovery gap could be 5% or 25% — too wide to be actionable
open_questions: What is the actual mark-to-recovery gap?
Which spillover channel activates first?
Is there a true historical analogue at $1.7T scale?
What is the actual default rate vs reported in private credit?

definition: The sequencing of credit deterioration → equity decline → labor market weakening with 3-12 month leads is empirically robust across the 1990, 2000-01, and 2007-09 episodes. Grounded in both New Keynesian (external finance premium is forward-looking, labor is lagging) and Post-Keynesian (endogenous money creation → spending → income → profits → equity validation → labor adjustment) frameworks.
evidence: Documented across 2000-01, 2007-09, and 1990 episodes
KB entry at confidence 9
Theoretically grounded in both New Keynesian and Post-Keynesian frameworks
Credit-equity linkage analysis provides the structural mechanism for this previously empirical observation
open_questions: Has the sequencing timing changed with structural market evolution since 2010?
Does private credit opacity delay the credit signal in this sequence?

definition: The Merton/KMV model explains 50-70% of cross-sectional CDS spread variation but is necessary-but-insufficient for credit-equity linkage: systematic Type I errors of 40-60%, underpredicts IG spreads by 50-100bp (credit spread puzzle), and is mechanically irrelevant for ~35% of S&P 500 index weight held by net-cash mega-caps. Useful for within-regime relative value but dangerous if treated as complete, particularly at regime transitions where it fails precisely when needed most.
evidence: KMV-Merton explains 50-70% of cross-sectional CDS spread variation (Bharath & Shumway 2008)
Systematic Type I errors of 40-60%
Credit spread puzzle: underpredicts IG by 50-100bp
Mechanically irrelevant for ~35% of S&P 500 held by net-cash mega-caps
All debates converged on useful foundation, dangerous if treated as complete
open_questions: What combination of Merton and Minsky/regime frameworks produces optimal credit-equity signal?
How should the model be adapted for the covenant-lite, addback-distorted universe?

definition: A zone of approximately 450-600bp in HY OAS exists where credit stress becomes self-reinforcing as primary market access shuts down, CLO arbitrage economics invert, and the maturity wall becomes a solvency wall. The precise level is regime-dependent, not a fixed point estimate. The Kalecki fiscal buffer may currently elevate the threshold by 100-150bp, but this higher threshold is itself destabilizing because more damage accumulates before the signal fires.
evidence: All specific point estimates refuted for false precision across multiple debates
Directional claim survived: above some spread level primary market shuts down
CLO arbitrage economics invert above threshold
Only 4 of 11 episodes where HY crossed 500bp produced >20% equity drawdowns
Signal is largely redundant with recession indicators
Kalecki fiscal buffer may elevate threshold by 100-150bp
Breadth of widening and banking system involvement matter more than absolute level
open_questions: What is the current regime-adjusted threshold given CLO market structure?
Is the signal redundant with recession indicators?
How does the Kalecki elevation of the threshold change the risk calculus?

definition: CLO overcollateralization test mechanics create a discrete, non-linear contagion channel: when CCC-bucket holdings breach the 7.5% threshold, cash flow diverts from equity to senior tranches, simultaneously destroying CLO equity returns, forcing leveraged loan selling, and shutting down new CLO formation (removing ~65-70% of leveraged loan demand). This is a step function, not a smooth degradation.
evidence: Identified as the single most specific and actionable contagion mechanism across all 8 analyses
7.5% CCC threshold triggers step function not smooth degradation
~$1T CLO market makes this systemically relevant
CLO formation represents ~65-70% of leveraged loan demand
PIK toggle usage at 5-8% up from 2% in 2022 as observable leading indicator
open_questions: What is current CCC-bucket utilization across the CLO market (4% = comfortable vs 6.5% = near cascade)?
How many downgrade waves would breach the threshold?
Data exists in Intex and LCD but is not freely available

definition: CDX HY open interest growth from ~$200B to ~$900B and credit ETFs at ~$400B create reverse causality: equity vol rises → managers buy CDX protection or short HYG as proxy hedge → mechanical spread widening → analysts cite credit stress → equity investors sell → confirmation loop. Supported by intraday evidence showing CDX lags SPX by 5-30 minutes during stress. Dealer inventory decline from ~$250B to ~$30-40B amplifies non-fundamental flow impact. A significant portion of high-frequency credit-equity correlation may be equity-to-credit, not credit-to-equity.
evidence: CDX HY open interest grown from ~$200B to ~$900B
Credit ETFs at ~$400B
Intraday evidence: CDX lags SPX by 5-30 minutes during stress
Largest ETF outflows correlate with equity vol spikes not credit-specific events
Dealer inventory decline from ~$250B to ~$30-40B amplifies non-fundamental flow impact
Pair_3 debate identified this as important novel insight
open_questions: What portion of high-frequency credit-equity correlation is equity-to-credit vs credit-to-equity?
Does the reverse causality channel invalidate longer-horizon (multi-month) credit leading indicators?
Can fundamental vs flow-driven spread moves be decomposed in real time?

definition: $350-500B of BBB-rated bonds at elevated downgrade risk represent a high-impact near-term transmission channel. BBB is the largest single rating cohort (~$3.5-4.0T), and IG-mandated funds (~$2T AUM) must sell within 30-90 days post-downgrade, creating concentrated supply shocks in HY markets. Fallen angel equity reprices 15-30% around downgrade events. Ford/GM 2005 template: $200B+ moving from IG to HY created multi-month dislocation and 100-150bp HY spread widening.
evidence: BBB is largest single rating cohort at ~$3.5-4.0T
IG-mandated funds ~$2T AUM must sell within 30-90 days post-downgrade
Fallen angel equity reprices 15-30% around downgrade events
Ford/GM 2005 template: $200B+ created multi-month dislocation and 100-150bp HY spread widening
Pair_1 debate flagged this as a glaring omission by credit_analyst_02
Not challenged by any agent
open_questions: Which specific BBB issuers are most at risk of downgrade?
How has market capacity to absorb fallen angels changed since 2005?
Would CLO structures amplify or absorb the supply shock?

definition: The Bernanke-Gertler-Gilchrist financial accelerator amplifies credit-equity transmission by approximately 1.5-2x and is currently loaded because EBITDA addback distortion means true leverage is higher than model calibrations suggest, implying a narrower distance-to-default and higher external finance premium elasticity than observable data indicate. Both NK and Post-Keynesian frameworks converge on this conclusion.
evidence: Embedded in Fed DSGE models (FRB/US, SIGMA)
1.5-2x amplification factor from calibration exercises
EBITDA addback distortion means accelerator is more loaded than baseline calibrations suggest
True leverage of 7.5-8.5x implies narrower distance-to-default
Both NK and Post-Keynesian frameworks arrive at same conclusion: feedback loop is primed
open_questions: What is the true accelerator coefficient with addback-adjusted leverage?
Can the amplification factor be directly measured rather than calibrated?

definition: Banking channel involvement is the key discriminant of credit-equity episode severity. When the banking channel is active (1990, 2007-08), median equity drawdown is -38% over 18-30 months. When inactive (1998, 2015-16, 2020), median drawdown is -22% over 3-6 months. Monitoring bank equity and bank CDS relative to HY widening distinguishes contained from systemic episodes in real time.
evidence: Banking channel active: median equity drawdown -38%, duration 18-30 months
Banking channel inactive: median equity drawdown -22%, duration 3-6 months
Confirmed as most actionable insight in pair_0 debate
Operational signpost: if regional bank equities fall in sympathy with HY widening, 1990/2007 template is activating
If HY widens but bank equity is stable, 2015-16 contained episode template
open_questions: Can banking channel activation be detected early enough to be actionable?
Does CRE exposure concentration in regional banks constitute current banking channel loading?
How do 13-14% CET1 ratios change the banking channel dynamics vs historical episodes?

definition: Term premium (not rate level) is a primary common discount-rate channel affecting both credit and equity simultaneously. Term-premium-driven yield rises hit credit and equity without growth offset, unlike rate-expectations-driven rises. Supply-driven term premium from post-2020 fiscal expansion ($2T+ annual net issuance) is a qualitatively different and worse type of tightening than policy-driven, with no growth offset.
evidence: Confirmed empirically by 2013 Taper Tantrum, 2018 Q4, 2023 Q3 — all produced synchronized credit-equity weakness
Supply-driven term premium is qualitatively different from policy-driven with no growth offset
Tripartite decomposition: growth-driven, policy-driven, supply-driven term premium
Partially supported in pair_3 debate
open_questions: Does mechanism operate on shrinking share of credit universe due to private credit migration?
How to decompose observed term premium moves into growth vs policy vs supply components in real time?

definition: The Treasury basis trade ($800B-$1T at 50-100x leverage) creates a novel cross-market contagion channel: unwind triggers Treasury selling → swap spread widening → higher funding costs → reduced dealer liquidity → wider credit bid-asks → forced selling by levered credit funds → VIX spike → equity risk-parity deleveraging. Fundamentally alters the flight-to-quality dynamic by converting the Treasury market from safe haven to contagion source.
evidence: Partially validated by September 2019 repo crisis and March 2020 Treasury dysfunction
Treasury market becomes contagion source rather than safe haven
Identified as rates_strategist_01's strongest unique contribution in pair_3 debate
$800B-$1T estimated size at 50-100x leverage
open_questions: What is the current size and leverage of the basis trade?
Has post-2020 regulatory attention reduced the risk?
Could cross-margining requirements trigger credit margin calls from Treasury unwinds?

definition: The Federal Reserve's reaction function includes a de facto credit spread term formalized by Curdia-Woodford (2010), where approximately 100bp HY spread widening equates to 25-50bp FFR tightening equivalent. This partially endogenizes the HY contagion threshold: the Fed will likely ease before primary markets shut down, unless inflation blocks the response. The Gilchrist-Zakrajsek Excess Bond Premium predicts GDP at 1-4 quarters with R-squared of 15-25%.
evidence: Curdia-Woodford (2010) formalization
~100bp HY spread widening approximates 25-50bp FFR tightening equivalent
Fed revealed behavior consistent: 2018 Q4 pivot, March 2020 emergency response, 2023 pause
Gilchrist-Zakrajsek Excess Bond Premium predicts GDP at 1-4 quarters with R² of 15-25%
If spreads widen 200bp, expect 50-100bp of shadow easing from Fed
open_questions: Does inflation constraint currently block the Fed's credit-spread response function?
How quickly can the Fed respond to credit stress given current political environment?
Is the implicit credit term sufficient to prevent primary market shutdowns during the maturity wall?

definition: The traditional bank credit impulse remains theoretically valid via the IS-curve mechanism but has structurally degraded as a US equity leading indicator, with R-squared declining from 0.38 (1975-2007) to 0.12 (2010-2025). The shift reflects bank credit-to-GDP flattening since 2008, corporate bond market doubling, private credit growing from $200B to $1.7T, and buybacks averaging ~$800B/yr. Still captures the SME/consumer channel (~40% of economy) and may predict macro recessions even if not S&P 500 returns.
evidence: IS-curve mechanism is textbook and BIS-validated
Bank credit-to-GDP flat since 2008
Corporate bond market doubled, private credit from $200B to $1.7T
Buybacks averaging ~$800B/yr mask credit channel
Currently sending divergent signal from equity pricing — decelerated while equities expanded
open_questions: Does credit impulse still predict macro recessions even if not S&P 500 returns?
Can decomposition into bank vs non-bank components restore predictive power?
What is the correct lag specification post-GFC (12-18 months vs 6 months)?
What is the unmeasured $1.7T private credit impulse doing?
